$source: CDC Travelers' Health country pages. See stages/01-data/references/cdc-raw.md.

$tier_model.required_vaccines: Entry requirements that apply regardless of origin country. Almost always empty for our 10 launch countries.
$tier_model.conditional_requirements: Entry requirements that apply only from certain origins (e.g. Yellow Fever if arriving from a YF-risk country). Each entry: {vaccine_id, condition}.
$tier_model.recommended_most_vaccines: CDC 'Recommended' without activity conditions. Applies to all travellers to this country.
$tier_model.recommended_some_vaccines: CDC 'Consider' or activity-conditional. Depends on itinerary, duration, activities.
$tier_model.routine_excluded: Routine vaccines (is_routine: true in vaccines.json) are not listed per country. Country pages reference the shared routine section.

$current_alerts_policy.purpose: Time-sensitive CDC travel health notices and current outbreak/flooding alerts. Rendered as a prominent banner on country pages, distinct from the stable health_notes field.
$current_alerts_policy.fields_per_alert: id (slug), title (short), detail (1-2 sentences), date_added (ISO), source (short phrase).
$current_alerts_policy.expiry_convention: Review every alert at least quarterly. Remove or refresh any alert older than 6 months unless confirmed still current on the CDC country page.

$entry_requirements_note: Visa and passport-validity data is sourced where verified; null means unverified — Stage 04 must add a 'check your government's guidance' note.

countries.name: Thailand

conditional_requirements.condition: If arriving from a country with risk of yellow fever transmission. Proof required for travellers aged 9 months and older; includes airport transits over 12 hours.

countries.malaria_note: Risk in rural border provinces with Myanmar, Cambodia (except Buri Ram), and Malaysia. No risk in Bangkok, Chiang Mai, Chiang Rai, Phuket, Koh Samui, Koh Pha Ngan, or Krabi islands. Most city and resort travellers don't need malaria pills.

entry_requirements.visa_note: No visa required for US citizens staying 60 days or less.

countries.health_notes: Standard tropical travel precautions apply — food and water hygiene and mosquito-bite prevention are the main defences against the risks that are not vaccine-preventable.

current_alerts.title: Southern Thailand flooding
current_alerts.detail: CDC reports land travel may be dangerous in flood zones, with elevated risk of waterborne, vector-borne, and fungal diseases.

countries.name: India

conditional_requirements.condition: If arriving from a country with risk of yellow fever transmission. Travellers aged 9 months and older without a valid certificate can be detained for up to 6 days.

countries.malaria_note: Risk throughout the country, including Mumbai and New Delhi. No risk above 2,000 m, which covers parts of Arunachal Pradesh, Himachal Pradesh, Jammu and Kashmir, Ladakh, Sikkim, and higher areas of Kerala and Tamil Nadu. Most travellers to lowland India should take malaria pills.

entry_requirements.visa_note: All US citizens require a visa before arrival.

countries.health_notes: India has elevated chikungunya risk and widespread dog-transmitted rabies. CDC has flagged counterfeit ABHAYRAB rabies vaccine circulating in Delhi — only get rabies shots at reputable hospitals if exposed.

countries.name: Mexico
countries.malaria_note: Risk in parts of Campeche, Chiapas, and southern Chihuahua. Rare cases in Oaxaca, Sinaloa, Sonora, and Tabasco. No risk along the US border or in resort areas of the Yucatán coast.

entry_requirements.visa_note: No visa required for US citizens staying up to 180 days.

countries.health_notes: Food and water precautions matter — multidrug-resistant Salmonella has been identified in returning travellers. Dog rabies is present but not widespread.

current_alerts.title: Rocky Mountain Spotted Fever in northern Mexico
current_alerts.detail: Illness and death due to RMSF reported across Baja California, Sonora, Chihuahua, Coahuila, and Nuevo León. Use insect-bite precautions.

current_alerts.title: New World Screwworm Myiasis
current_alerts.detail: Cases reported across Mexico and Central America in areas where it had been eliminated. Avoid bug bites, keep open wounds clean and covered, and avoid time around livestock.

current_alerts.title: Multidrug-resistant Salmonella Newport
current_alerts.detail: Travellers returning from Mexico have been infected. Follow food and water precautions.

countries.name: Indonesia

conditional_requirements.condition: If arriving from a country with risk of yellow fever transmission. Proof required for travellers aged 9 months and older.

countries.malaria_note: Risk across eastern Indonesia (Maluku, North Maluku, East Nusa Tenggara, Papua, West Papua — including Labuan Bajo and Komodo) and rural Kalimantan, Sulawesi, Sumatra, Lombok. No malaria risk in Jakarta, Bali resorts, Ubud, Gili Islands, or Java resort areas.
countries.health_notes: Dog-transmitted rabies is common across the country. Dengue and other mosquito-borne viruses are a year-round risk in the tropics.

current_alerts.title: Major flooding across Indonesian islands
current_alerts.detail: Land travel may be dangerous in flood zones and healthcare infrastructure has been damaged in affected areas.

current_alerts.title: Increased human rabies cases in West Timor
current_alerts.detail: CDC reports a rise in human rabies cases in West Timor. Avoid contact with dogs and other animals, and seek immediate care for any bite or scratch.

countries.name: Vietnam
countries.malaria_note: Rural areas only, with rare cases in the Mekong and Red River deltas. No malaria risk in Hanoi, Ho Chi Minh City, Da Nang, Hai Phong, Nha Trang, or Quy Nhon. Most tourist itineraries do not need malaria pills.
countries.health_notes: Dengue is a year-round risk across much of Vietnam, with larger outbreaks every 2–5 years. Mosquito-bite prevention matters even when a vaccine isn't advised. CDC does not currently advise the chikungunya vaccine for travel to Vietnam.

countries.name: Japan

entry_requirements.visa_note: No visa required for US citizens staying up to 90 days.

countries.health_notes: Japan has the lowest infectious-disease risk of our featured destinations. CDC lists Hepatitis A only for street-food enthusiasts or rural travellers, not for most city itineraries. Dog rabies is not common in Japan. Tick-borne encephalitis is present — avoid tick exposure if you'll be hiking, but CDC does not recommend the vaccine for travel to Japan.

countries.name: Kenya

conditional_requirements.condition: If arriving from a country with risk of yellow fever transmission. Proof required for travellers aged 1 year and older.

countries.malaria_note: Risk across the country below 2,500 m, including all safari parks. Rare cases in central urban Nairobi. Most travellers — including everyone on a safari itinerary — should take malaria pills. Seek medical attention for any fever during or within a year of travel.

entry_requirements.visa_note: US citizens require a visa before arrival.

countries.health_notes: CDC recommends yellow fever vaccine for most of Kenya. It is not advised if your travel is limited to Nairobi, the former North Eastern Province (Mandera, Wajir, Garissa), or most of the former Coast Province (except Taita-Taveta).

current_alerts.title: Active cholera transmission in specific counties
current_alerts.detail: CDC reports active cholera transmission in Garissa, Kisumu, Kwale, Migori, Mombasa, Nairobi, Narok, and Turkana counties. Cholera is rare in travellers; safe food, water, and handwashing remain the main protection.

countries.name: Egypt

conditional_requirements.condition: If arriving from a country with risk of yellow fever transmission. Proof required for travellers aged 9 months and older; includes airport transits over 12 hours.

countries.health_notes: No malaria risk. Schistosomiasis is a real risk in the Nile and other fresh water — do not swim or wade in freshwater. Dog rabies is common; rabies shots may only be available at larger urban hospitals if you're exposed.

countries.name: Peru
countries.malaria_note: Risk across the country below 2,500 m east of the Andes, including Iquitos and Puerto Maldonado. Rare cases in Tumbes and Piura. No malaria risk in Lima, Arequipa, Cusco, Machu Picchu, Lake Titicaca, or the Pacific coast.
countries.health_notes: Yellow fever is recommended for travel to the Amazon regions below 2,300 m — Amazonas, Cusco, Huánuco, Junín, Loreto, Madre de Dios, Pasco, Puno, San Martín, Ucayali, and parts of the northern highlands. Not needed for trips limited to Lima, Cusco city, the Inca Trail, Machu Picchu, or the Pacific coast.

current_alerts.title: Oropouche cases in the Americas
current_alerts.detail: CDC reports low numbers of Oropouche cases across several countries in the region. Spread by midges and mosquitoes; take bite precautions.

current_alerts.title: Major flooding across Peru
current_alerts.detail: Multiple districts experiencing major flooding with elevated risk of waterborne disease including leptospirosis. Land travel may be dangerous in flood zones.

countries.name: Philippines

conditional_requirements.condition: If arriving from a country with risk of yellow fever transmission. Proof required for travellers aged 9 months and older; includes airport transits over 12 hours.

countries.malaria_note: Risk in Palawan and the Mindanao island group. No risk in metropolitan Manila or other urban areas. Most tourist itineraries focused on Cebu, Bohol, or Metro Manila do not need malaria pills.
countries.health_notes: Dog rabies is common across the country. The Philippines FDA has warned of counterfeit Speeda rabies vaccine and Equirab antiserum in circulation — get rabies shots only at reputable hospitals if exposed. Cholera is presumed present; stick to safe food and water.

countries.name: Turkey

conditional_requirements.condition: If arriving from a country with risk of yellow fever transmission. Proof required for travellers aged 1 year and older.

countries.malaria_note: No active malaria transmission in Turkey. Historic risk in southeastern provinces is no longer considered significant by CDC.
countries.health_notes: Standard food and water precautions apply, especially outside large hotels. Stray dog populations are common in many cities, which is the main reason rabies may come up. West Nile virus and tick-borne diseases are present in summer; sensible mosquito and tick precautions matter if you spend time outdoors May to October.

countries.name: Brazil

conditional_requirements.condition: If arriving from a country with risk of yellow fever transmission. Proof required for travellers aged 9 months and older.

countries.malaria_note: Risk in the Amazon Basin states (Acre, Amapá, Amazonas, Maranhão, Mato Grosso, Pará, Rondônia, Roraima, and Tocantins). No risk in Rio de Janeiro, São Paulo, the eastern coastal beaches, or Iguaçu Falls.
countries.health_notes: Standard tropical travel precautions apply. Dengue, Zika, and chikungunya circulate year-round across much of Brazil; mosquito-bite prevention matters even where vaccines aren't advised.

countries.name: Morocco

conditional_requirements.condition: If arriving from a country with risk of yellow fever transmission. Proof required for travellers aged 1 year and older.

countries.malaria_note: No active malaria transmission in Morocco.
countries.health_notes: Standard food and water precautions apply, especially outside large hotels and riads. Stray dogs and animals are common in cities and rural areas, which is the main reason rabies may come up.

countries.name: United Arab Emirates

conditional_requirements.condition: If arriving from a country with risk of yellow fever transmission. Proof required for travellers aged 1 year and older.

countries.malaria_note: No active malaria transmission in the UAE.
countries.health_notes: Modern medical infrastructure; standard food and water precautions are sensible outside major hotels. Travellers continuing on to Saudi Arabia for Hajj or Umrah should also discuss meningococcal vaccination with their doctor.

countries.name: Tanzania

conditional_requirements.condition: If arriving from a country with risk of yellow fever transmission, including airport transits over 12 hours. Proof required for travellers aged 1 year and older.

countries.malaria_note: Risk throughout the country below 1,800 metres, including all major safari parks (Serengeti, Ngorongoro, Tarangire, Lake Manyara) and Zanzibar. Most travellers should take malaria pills, and any fever during or within a year of travel needs medical attention.
countries.health_notes: Standard food and water precautions apply. Schistosomiasis is a risk in fresh water — avoid swimming in lakes and rivers including Lake Victoria.

countries.name: South Africa

conditional_requirements.condition: If arriving from a country with risk of yellow fever transmission, including airport transits over 12 hours. Proof required for travellers aged 1 year and older.

countries.malaria_note: Risk in low-altitude areas of Limpopo and Mpumalanga (including Kruger National Park) and northeastern KwaZulu-Natal, mainly from October to May. No risk in Cape Town, the Garden Route, the Western Cape, or Johannesburg.
countries.health_notes: Standard food and water precautions apply. Tuberculosis and HIV are common in the general population — relevant context if you may need medical or dental care during your stay.

countries.name: Costa Rica

conditional_requirements.condition: If arriving from a country with risk of yellow fever transmission. Proof required for travellers aged 9 months and older.

countries.malaria_note: Limited risk in some remote eastern lowland areas; most tourist itineraries (San José, Arenal, Manuel Antonio, Monteverde, Guanacaste beaches) do not need malaria pills.
countries.health_notes: Standard tropical travel precautions apply. Dengue circulates year-round across most of the country; mosquito-bite prevention is the main protection against the risks that aren't vaccine-preventable.

countries.name: Sri Lanka

conditional_requirements.condition: If arriving from a country with risk of yellow fever transmission. Proof required for travellers aged 9 months and older.

countries.malaria_note: No active malaria transmission in Sri Lanka. WHO certified the country malaria-free in 2016.
countries.health_notes: Standard food and water precautions apply outside large hotels. Dengue is significant year-round, particularly during and after monsoon seasons; mosquito-bite prevention matters.
